danger_signals.visual_form: Goalkeeper animated holding ball with player approaching
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent scoring opportunity; goalkeeper vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Clustering of player sprites near own goal
danger_signals.meaning: Defensive pressure; risk of conceding goal

safety_signals.visual_form: Ball sprite attached to controlled player
Isometric spatial positioning on field grid
safety_signals.meaning: Safe distance; low immediate threat to own goal

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: National flags displayed for team identification
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team rendered in contrasting kit colors; skin tone variation indicates regional confederation membership
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stadium environment same for all teams; national identity signaled purely through player sprite appearance and flags

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Regional confederation badge (implied through team selection menus)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Teams: China, Hong Kong, Iraq, Israel, Japan, Qatar, Turkey
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player skin tone darker for certain AFC teams; no environmental differentiation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Teams: Algeria, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Morocco, Nigeria
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player sprite skin tone significantly darker; uniform visual distinction from other confederations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Teams: England, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, etc. (22 teams total)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player sprites with lighter skin tone; largest faction group with 24 national teams

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Teams: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Uruguay
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Geographic region indicated through team selection only; no environmental visual cues
Limited regional representation (3 teams); no environmental differentiation
Minimal representation (2 teams); no environmental visual distinction

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Fictional team with generic player appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: No geographic or political affiliation; purely corporate entity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Attacking team players animated in forward motion; ball sprite moving toward opponent goal; isometric camera tracks ball position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Controlled player passes/shoots; AI teammates move into attacking positions; defenders retreat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defending team players clustered near own goal; ball sprite in opponent possession; goal and goalkeeper prominently displayed on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Controlled defender moves to intercept; AI teammates form defensive wall; goalkeeper repositions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goalkeeper sprite animated in specific pose holding ball; player sprite positioned in immediate proximity in penalty area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI goalkeeper automatically kicks ball to nearest opponent; easy goal-scoring opportunity emerges (design oversight)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Menu-based state change; visual feedback through tournament bracket display showing team progression or elimination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Eliminated teams greyed out or removed from bracket; advancing teams highlight; group stage transitions to knockout stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display prominently shown; crowd celebration/disappointment animations; team kit colors associated with winning/losing state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Crowd sound effects reflect outcome; player animation loops change (celebration vs. dejection poses)

environmental_storytelling.example: Each team identified by their national flag in team selection screens and match interface; flags communicate political/geographic identity without explicit text
Home vs. away kits in contrasting colors signal team identity and opposition; primary visual method of faction recognition during isometric match play

environmental_storytelling.technique: Skin tone variation as regional marker
environmental_storytelling.example: African confederation (CAF) teams rendered with darker player skin tones; Asian, European, South American teams with lighter tones; crude but intentional regional visual classification
Ambient crowd reactions communicate match excitement and outcome significance; audio world-state indicator without visual representation
Tournament mode mimics FIFA World Cup structure with group stages and knockout rounds; bracket progression visually communicates escalating stakes and match importance
Standardized stadium environment for all nations; no environmental customization; visual statement that all teams compete on equal footing despite geopolitical differences

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Holding ball in hands (prepared to clear)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Automatically kicks ball to nearest player (often opposing player if one positioned nearby); exposes team to immediate goal danger
Repositions based on ball location; animates catching/throwing motions; reactive to attacking pressure
Responds to player input commands; passes, shoots, moves with direct responsiveness (noted as slower than ideal by critics)
Moves into attacking positions; makes runs toward opponent goal; attempts to receive passes
Moves to block passes and shots; clusters near own goal; attempts to intercept ball
Generates ambient noise; reacts audibly to goals and significant plays; sound design conveys match energy and stakes

notes: FIFA International Soccer uses minimal visual signaling for world-state and faction communication, relying primarily on team kit colors and national flags for identity. The game contains no explicit danger/safety UI indicators; world-state communication is behavioral (through player animation and positioning) rather than symbolic. The isometric viewpoint and standardized stadium environment create a politically neutral presentation despite featuring 48 national teams across multiple confederations. Skin tone variation in player sprites is the only visual technique differentiating regional confederations, reflecting 1993 game design conventions. The game's manifestation layer is sparse compared to modern standards—world-state and faction identity are communicated through diegetic elements (player appearance, ball position, crowd audio) rather than explicit HUD systems.
